name: Rouge d'Hiver
description: A French heirloom winter romaine with upright, bronze-red leaves that deepen in color with cold. Exceptionally cold-hardy with a crisp texture and slightly nutty, complex flavor.
history: Rouge d'Hiver means 'red of winter' in French — named for its ability to be grown through autumn and early winter in French kitchen gardens. Documented in French seed catalogs since the 19th century.

good.reason: Similar cold preferences, efficient bed use

seasonal_quirks: Color deepens from green-bronze to deep red-burgundy as temperatures drop. Best sown in late summer for fall and winter harvest. One of the most cold-tolerant lettuces — survives temperatures down to 20°F with protection.
harvest_cues: Heads should be upright and firm, 8–10 inches tall. Outer leaves can be harvested at any stage. Best flavor when nights are cold.
variety_notes: One of the few lettuces that improves in flavor as temperatures drop. Ideal for succession planting into late fall. Paired with Little Gem, gives a range of textures and colors for a winter salad garden.
preservation: Stores in cold frames or refrigerator. Does not freeze.
